title: Current-build playtest closure
model: Use the current efficient coding model with medium reasoning
prompt: Work only on issues found during the owner's current Pocket Potion Works playtest. Read AGENTS.md, README.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, and the active release/audio documentation. Do not read archive/. Preserve the charcoal-black and purple theme, current save compatibility, core loop, and fake-only platform boundaries. Fix only confirmed playtest issues, add focused regression coverage, run npm.cmd run check, and verify affected behavior at 390x844 and 360x740. Do not publish, add production services, or begin the next roadmap phase.
acceptance: Owner-reported current-build issues are resolved without unrelated feature expansion
Gather, brew, collect, deliver, and upgrade remain completable
Audio cue assignment and mix match owner decisions
Exact-size browser QA has no horizontal overflow or console errors
Current release evidence and documentation match the tested build

title: Potion mastery and recipe discovery
model: Use the current efficient coding model with medium-high reasoning
prompt: After Task 6 and owner approval, design a small deterministic potion-mastery and recipe-discovery system. Keep it data-driven, offline, save-compatible, and understandable on mobile. Add meaningful recipe-specific progression without random dead ends, impossible orders, production services, or a large content dump. Present the design and migration plan for review before broad implementation.
acceptance: Mastery adds a meaningful goal beyond player level
Existing saves migrate without losing progress
Order generation remains completable
Progression is covered by deterministic tests and economy simulation
Owner approves the scope before further content expansion

title: Upgrade choices and customer relationships
model: Use the current efficient coding model with medium-high reasoning
prompt: After mastery is approved, add clearer upgrade previews and a lightweight recurring-customer relationship system. Preserve short sessions, offline play, deterministic saves, accessibility, and mobile layout. Favor a few meaningful decisions and short request chains over large amounts of text or content.
acceptance: Upgrade purchases show their next-level effect before confirmation
At least two upgrade paths create a meaningful choice
Recurring customers provide visible progress without blocking the core loop
No live service, account, or generated dialogue dependency is introduced

title: Offline-friendly retention and customization
model: Use the current efficient coding model with medium reasoning
prompt: After the progression systems are approved, prototype workshop cosmetics, weekly request chains, and collection goals that work fully offline and do not punish missed days. Keep scope small, save-compatible, and measurable through deterministic tests and short owner playtests.
acceptance: Retention goals work without an account or server clock
Missing a day does not cause irreversible loss or punishment
Cosmetic rewards do not alter required progression balance
Save migration and rollback behavior are documented and tested

title: Public beta decision
model: Use the current efficient coding model with high reasoning
prompt: After owner approval of the gameplay MVP, perform a fresh public-PWA beta audit. Test real install, update, offline relaunch, storage behavior, target browsers/devices, current disclosures, screenshots, support/privacy requirements, and rollback. Do not publish or connect production monetization, analytics, accounts, or cloud services without explicit owner authorization.
acceptance: Public beta verdict is evidence-backed and target-specific
Current screenshots and disclosures match the approved build
Install, update, offline relaunch, and rollback are tested in real target environments
Publishing remains an explicit owner action
